step_by_step_reasoning: We identify six nodes based on the entities listed in the World Model: Fire Hydrant (UserConsumerIOT), API GATEWAY (ApiGateway), S3 (S3), ECS (ECS), SAGEMAKER (SageMaker), and ANALYST (UserCompanyAnalyst). We build a single continuous pipeline (flow_id 0) from the visual connections specified. It starts with acoustic data from the Fire Hydrant being ingested into API GATEWAY (seq 0), stored in S3 (seq 1), processed as daily batches by ECS (seq 2), and classified by SageMaker (seq 3). Although ANALYST is present in the entities, no visual connection to it is listed in the World Model, so we include it as a node but exclude any connected edges.

graph.name: Muller Water Products - Intelligent Water Platform
graph.notes: An intelligent water network monitoring system that ingests acoustic data from smart fire hydrants via API Gateway to S3. A batch processing architecture in ECS runs intensive acoustic algorithms, sending results to SageMaker to calculate leak probability scores.

nodes.name: Fire Hydrant
nodes.notes: Smart fire hydrant IoT devices that collect acoustic signals daily to detect pipe leaks.

nodes.name: API GATEWAY
nodes.notes: Handles secure ingestion and authentication of the acoustic signal data files.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: Acts as the data lake, persisting the daily uploaded acoustic signal files.

nodes.name: ECS
nodes.notes: Runs containerized, processor-intensive batch processes on a daily basis to analyze node-pair correlations.

nodes.name: SAGEMAKER
nodes.notes: Classifies persistent correlations to assign a leak probability score using ML models.

nodes.name: ANALYST
nodes.notes: Human analyst team who reviews and qualifies the leak predictions.

edges.notes: Sensors send daily acoustic signal files to the ingestion endpoint.
Ingested files are persisted securely in S3 storage.
A daily batch process in ECS reads the acoustic data files from S3.
ECS sends the correlated acoustic signal data to SageMaker for ML classification.
